text: In the LapSim VAS module, the surgeon uses endosteplars to sequentially divide the vessels, fissures, and bronchus while dissecting and mobilizing the upper right lung lobe for complete lobe resection.
Structures are prepared with sponge sticks or if you like other instruments selected from the menu.

The surgeon uses thoracoscopic forceps introduced through the posterior port to pass behind the superior pulmonary vein, following the identification of the middle lobe vein. The superior pulmonary vein is encircled with a vessel rubber loop, creating space for a stapler to be introduced around the vessel.
The surgeon introduces an endovascular stapler through the posterior port, passing its thin blade behind the superior pulmonary vein. The vein is divided after the removal of the vessel loop.
After dividing the pulmonary vein, the surgeon visualizes the pulmonary artery and truncus anterior. The minor fissure is then divided using endosteplars.

Following the transection of the fissure past the artery, the middle lobe descends, revealing the posterior part of the artery and the remaining arterial branches to the upper lobe, which are subsequently transected.
The surgeon removes the complete upper lobe using an endobag, extracting it through the utility incision.
To see the complete exercise, please contact Surgical Science.
